text.title: ES Aggregate Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding the event-sourced aggregate pattern.

rule.statement: Aggregate state MUST be mutated only inside `apply` / `_apply_<EventName>` event-handler methods. Command methods MUST validate invariants, append events to a pending list, and return. Direct field assignment in command methods is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mutation in command methods makes replay diverge from live execution; rebuilding state from events would produce a different result.

rule.statement: Each command method MUST emit zero or more events and return (no value). It MUST NOT call the event store directly. Persistence is the repository's responsibility, called after the command returns.
rule.rationale: Calling the store from inside the aggregate couples domain logic to infrastructure and prevents pure unit tests.

rule.statement: Repository `save(stream_id, events, expected_version)` MUST always pass the version loaded at command start. The store MUST raise `ConcurrencyError` (or equivalent) when versions disagree.
rule.rationale: Omitting expected_version silently overwrites concurrent commits; data corruption is invisible until replay diverges.

rule.statement: The aggregate MUST expose a class method `from_events(stream_id, events) -> Aggregate` (or constructor accepting `events`) that replays through `apply` handlers in order. Live execution and replay MUST produce identical state.
rule.rationale: Without a single reconstruction path, projections and snapshots cannot trust the aggregate's state derivation.

rule.statement: The aggregate MUST expose `collect_pending_events()` that returns and clears new events. The repository calls this AFTER `save(...)` succeeds; if save raises, pending events MUST remain on the aggregate for retry.
rule.rationale: Collecting before save risks losing events on transient store failures; collecting after save preserves at-least-once semantics.
